stories.title: Your 'app' could have been a webpage (so I fixed it for you)
stories.titleZh: 你的“应用”本来可以只是个网页（所以我帮你改好了）

comments.author: OkayPhysicist
comments.textRawHtml:
Nobody here is talking about the fact that a significant number of users want apps, too.

I'm responsible for an internal tool at the company I work for, hosted as a website, that handles a bunch of miscellaneous tasks that other employees need. Think reimbursements, documentation and reporting, gathering and presenting business data. That sort of thing.

When I took it over, it was desktop only ( a lot of <table> formatted pages with fixed px sizes). I spruced it up, modernized it to work on screens of any size, and created a mobile version of any pages that just didn't translate well to small screens (think "large tables of information").

When I announced the update, the number of people who asked me variations of "how to get website on phone if website on computer" or requested I make the damn thing an app was outrageous.

We take tech literacy for granted, because it's like a dozen levels down fundamental to our entire field. But the tech illiterati exist, and they love apps.
comments.textZhHtml:
这里没有人谈论这样一个事实：大量用户也需要应用程序。

我负责我工作的公司的一个内部工具，以网站形式托管，用于处理其他员工需要的一堆杂项任务。考虑报销、文档和报告、收集和呈现业务数据。诸如此类的事情。

当我接手它时，它只是桌面版（很多具有固定 px 大小的 格式的页面）。我对它进行了改进，使其能够在任何尺寸的屏幕上工作，并创建了任何不能很好地适应小屏幕的页面的移动版本（想想“大信息表”）。

当我宣布更新时，有很多人问我“如果网站在计算机上，如何在手机上访问网站”或要求我制作一个应用程序，这真是太离谱了。

我们认为技术素养是理所当然的，因为它就像十几个级别对我们整个领域来说是根本性的。但技术文盲是存在的，而且他们喜欢应用程序。

> 我们认为技术素养是理所当然的，因为它对于我们整个领域来说就像是降低了十几个级别。但技术文盲是存在的，而且他们喜欢应用程序。

他们“喜欢应用程序”，因为苹果和安卓已经花费了数十亿美元来打破他们的思维模式，并说服他们“你使用应用程序在手机上做事”。 字面上地。 这是大多数人的理解程度。

所以，当然，他们“想要”应用程序就像早期互联网用户“想要”AOL 一样，因为在他们看来，AOL 和互联网是没有区别的。 但真正的自由选择需要对选择的理解。

这确实没有意义。经过数十年的网络使用后，应用程序立即非常流行。选择应用程序的理由有很多。即使在桌面上；如果我全天使用足够多的服务，我想要一个本机应用程序，而不是网站。
偏爱应用程序的原因有很多，其中大多数是操作系统制造商削弱网络应用程序的方式，而这些原因与如果用户选择本机应用程序则获得 30% 的分成完全无关。
此时，大多数“原生”应用程序实际上只是打包在 Electron 中的 Web 应用程序。您通常无法区分其中的差异，这一事实证明没有任何真正的理由来选择本机应用程序。所有原因都与操作系统供应商试图阻碍 PWA 或 Web 开发人员无缘无故地创建 React 怪物有关。 Web 应用程序实际上可以非常高效。
这是错误的。我喜欢移动应用程序的最重要原因与我喜欢桌面程序的原因相同。

- 我可以下载一次然后使用它。

- 我可以看到它何时更新（相对于每次加载时都会更新的网站）。出于安全原因，更好的是，我什至可以与其他人在线验证我的应用程序，并确保我们运行相同的东西。

- 端到端加密在网页中没有多大意义，因为我从根本上必须信任服务器（它为我每次运行的整个应用程序提供服务）。如果您关心端到端加密，那么您需要一个应用程序。

- 对于开源应用程序，我可以审核代码，可能会对其进行编辑并使用它。我无法通过网页做到这一点。

- 我只使用免费应用程序，所以 30% 的零是...零。

一个重要但未包括在内：网络应用程序是资源消耗者
是的，这个

children.author: palata
children.textRawHtml:
This is wrong. The most important reasons why I prefer mobile apps are the same as why I prefer desktop programs.

- I can download it once and then use it.

- I can see when it gets updated (versus a website that gets updated every time I load it). For security reasons it's better, I can even verify my app with other people online and make sure we run the same thing.

- End-to-end encryption doesn't make much sense in a webpage, because I fundamentally have to trust the server (which serves me the whole app I run every time). If you care about end-to-end encryption, you want an app.

- For open source apps, I can audit the code, possibly edit it, and use it. I cannot do that with a webpage.

- I exclusively use free apps, so 30% of zero is... zero.

children.author: palata
children.textRawHtml:
I understand your point, but I am not sure how relevant it is to use bad practice as a criticism of the technology itself.

Like I can write a modern C++ program that will be orders of magnitudes slower than an interpreted Java program from the 90s. But that does not mean that C++ is slower than interpreted Java: just that I can't write software, right?

If I care about security, then I choose apps that I believe are built correctly. I do happen to believe that ProtonMail is built correctly, and Signal as well. But it remains that because of how the technology works, the end-to-end encryption happening in ProtonMail requires me to trust the server, which defeats the point of end-to-end encryption in the first place [1].

[1]: to be fair, it does not defeat the purpose of ProtonMail entirely. It helps me trust that ProtonMail doesn't massively store data of all the users. But it is weaker because Proton can easily target me personally, and Signal cannot.
children.textZhHtml:
我理解你的观点，但我不确定用糟糕的实践来批评技术本身有多大相关性。

比如，我能写出现代C++程序，其速度比90年代的解释型Java程序慢好几个数量级。但这并不意味着C++比解释型Java慢：只能说明我不会写软件，对吧？

如果我关心安全性，那我会选择我认为构建得当的应用。我确实认为ProtonMail和Signal构建得很规范。但问题在于，由于技术的工作原理，ProtonMail中进行的端到端加密要求我必须信任服务器，这从一开始就违背了端到端加密的初衷[1]。

[1]：公平地说，这并没有完全否定ProtonMail的意义。它让我相信ProtonMail不会大规模存储所有用户的数据。但它确实更弱，因为Proton可以轻易地针对我个人，而Signal做不到。

children.author: bloppe
children.textRawHtml: At this point, most of those "native" apps are actually just web apps packaged in Electron. The fact that you generally can't tell the difference is proof that there aren't any real reasons to prefer a native app. All the reasons have to do with either OS vendors trying to hobble PWAs, or web developers creating React monsters for no good reason. Web apps can actually be very performant.
children.textZhHtml: 此时，大多数“原生”应用程序实际上只是打包在 Electron 中的 Web 应用程序。您通常无法区分其中的差异，这一事实证明没有任何真正的理由来选择本机应用程序。所有原因都与操作系统供应商试图阻碍 PWA 或 Web 开发人员无缘无故地创建 React 怪物有关。 Web 应用程序实际上可以非常高效。

children.author: gymhasanapp
children.textRawHtml:
The real reason is really simple tho, and we could identify it ahead of time even before apps existed:

The average users sucks at managing bookmarks.

Telling a user that a feature is a webpage instead of an app means that they, in their glorious tech illiterate brain, think they have to memorize the url. They don't want to do that. They want to "have it" so they don't forget it. And having it is easier in the app mental model to them than in the bookmark one.

I know sufficient 40+ year olds who have the most absolute disgraceful phone desktops, filled with garbage. And yet, this is what they want. They want their bookmarkrs in the one place they'll check. And they want no folders.
children.textZhHtml:
不过真正的原因其实很简单，甚至在应用出现之前我们就能提前预见到：

普通用户根本不擅长管理书签。

告诉用户某个功能是个网页而不是应用，意味着在他们那“出色”的科技盲大脑里，会觉得自己必须记住网址。他们不想这么做。他们想“拥有”它，这样就不会忘了。而在他们的心智模型中，通过应用来“拥有”它比通过书签容易得多。

我认识不少40多岁的人，他们的手机桌面简直惨不忍睹，塞满了各种垃圾。然而，这正是他们想要的。他们希望把书签放在自己唯一会去查看的地方。而且他们不要任何文件夹。

Very nice, multi modal, largest open weight model that supports audio. Would be interesting to see how good the audio capability is.

If you want to run locally, checkout https://github.com/danielhanchen/llama.cpp/tree/add-inkling https://unsloth.ai/docs/models/inkling https://huggingface.co/unsloth/inkling-GGUF https://huggingface.co/unsloth/inkling-NVFP4

This supposedly is better than KimiK2.7, as much hype as GLM5.2 gets, I find myself using KimiK2.7 half of the time, so if the benchmark is true, then this can definitely go in the mix. My hope is that it might have strengths in some areas to beat all other open weight models.

如果您想在本地运行，请查看 https://github.com/danielhanchen/llama.cpp/tree/add-inkling https://unsloth.ai/docs/models/inkling https://huggingface.co/unsloth/inkling-GGUF https://huggingface.co/unsloth/inkling-NVFP4

children.author: mark_l_watson
children.textRawHtml:
+1 I enthusiastically use Chinese open weight models for a wide range of tasks (I also love Opus and Gemini) but I am so happy to see another high quality American open model (I consider gemma to be high quality, like qwen).

I enjoyed turning off web search for Inkling to experiment with what innate knowledge is encoded in the model weights. A fun thing I do is check what innate knowledge very large models contain about me, as an individual. Inkling has an interesting concise shadow of what I do. (I have written a lot of books, so I am in training data.)
children.textZhHtml:
+1 我热衷于将中国开放权重模型用于各种任务（我也喜欢 Opus 和 Gemini），但我很高兴看到另一个高质量的美国开放模型（我认为 gemma 质量很高，比如 qwen）。

我喜欢关闭 Inkling 的网络搜索来试验模型权重中编码的先天知识。我做的一件有趣的事情是检查非常大的模型包含关于我作为个人的先天知识。 Inkling 对我所做的事情有一个有趣而简洁的影子。 （我写了很多书，所以我在训练数据。）

我还不知道它有多智能，但是 NVIDIA LLM 为快速推理进行了很好的优化（当然是在他们的 GPU 上）。

之前那是美国最大的开放权重 LLM。

children.author: throwaw12
children.textRawHtml:
What does it mean, if it is American?

Is it censored or will it eventually stop working in Middle Eastern countries?

Or is it biased towards powerful political lobby group interests?

When weights are open I usually don't care where is it from, as long as it is working for my use cases well
children.textZhHtml:
如果它是美国的，这意味着什么？

它是否受到审查，或者最终会在中东国家停止工作？

或者它是否偏向强大的政治游说团体利益？

当权重开放时，我通常不在乎它来自哪里，只要它适合我的用例即可

children.author: swiftcoder
children.textRawHtml: There's a lot of Sinophobia in the tech world, and the AI race seems to be magnifying that. With very little evidence to back it up, I might add - we've had regular releases of quality open-weight models from Chinese firms, and no sign they are more censored/ideological, than, say, Grok
children.textZhHtml: 科技界存在很多恐华情绪，而 AI 竞赛似乎正在放大这种情绪。而且我要补充一点，这几乎没有什么证据支持——中国企业一直在定期发布高质量的开源权重模型，也没有迹象表明它们比 Grok 等模型受到更多审查或更具意识形态色彩。

children.author: smiths1999
children.textRawHtml: If the company does any kind of work with the US Government it's far easier to just say no to Chinese-origin models rather than deal with all the overhead on the government contracts. Since just about every major tech company has US contracts they all have to be careful about the use of Chinese models.
children.textZhHtml: 如果公司与美国政府有任何形式的合作，直接拒绝使用中国背景的模型要比处理政府合同中所有的繁文缛节容易得多。由于几乎所有大型科技公司都有美国政府的合同，因此它们在使用中国模型时都必须非常谨慎。

children.author: mastermage
children.textRawHtml: Only actualy censored thing i found so far was Tianmen Square. And depending on what you use it for thats realy not a big issue.
children.textZhHtml: 到目前为止，我发现唯一真正被审查的内容是天安门广场。而且这取决于你的用途，这其实不是什么大问题。

stories.title: How to stop Claude from saying load-bearing
stories.titleZh: 如何让Claude停止使用“承重”一词

comments.author: doctoboggan
comments.textRawHtml:
I do not mind when I am coding with Claude and it uses all the typical claudisms. I am much more bothered when I am reading a blog post, email, or other form of prose and I see those same claudisms.

I guess they are not annoying since I know I am talking to an LLM and expect the typical responses. When I am reading prose online that I previously would have expected a human to write, it can be quite jarring to realize its an LLM.
comments.textZhHtml:
我不介意与 Claude 一起编码，它使用了所有典型的claudisms。当我阅读博客文章、电子邮件或其他形式的散文时，我会更加烦恼，并且看到同样的克劳斯。

我想他们并不烦人，因为我知道我正在与法学硕士交谈并期待典型的回应。当我在网上阅读我以前认为是人类写的散文时，意识到它是法学硕士可能会很不舒服。

children.author: rpdillon
children.textRawHtml: I didn't use Claude for a long time, but my coworkers did, so I got infected through a side channel: I ended up reading their vibed docs, noticed "load-bearing", kind of liked it, and started using it in conversation, until I got feedback that I was "talking like Claude", so now I avoid the phrase entirely. The intersection of language and social norms is interesting.
children.textZhHtml: 我很长一段时间没有使用 Claude，但我的同事使用了，所以我通过旁道被感染：我最终阅读了他们的 vibed 文档，注意到“承重”，有点喜欢它，并开始在对话中使用它，直到我得到反馈说我“像 Claude 一样说话”，所以现在我完全避免使用这个短语。语言和社会规范的交集很有趣。

children.author: cafebeen
children.textRawHtml: Yes, I had a related experience of reading a book and observing what I thought were claude-isms, only to realize it was written in 2019.  Some of the common tells are actually good writing practices, but I guess they are best in smaller doses.
children.textZhHtml: 是的，我有过读一本书并观察我认为是克劳德主义的相关经历，结果发现它是在 2019 年写的。一些常见的故事实际上是很好的写作习惯，但我想它们最好是小剂量的。

children.author: palmotea
children.textRawHtml:
> Yes, I had a related experience of reading a book and observing what I thought were claude-isms, only to realize it was written in 2019. Some of the common tells are actually good writing practices, but I guess they are best in smaller doses.

The LLMs haven't invented anything. Every LLM-ism is some pre-existing and established word, metaphor, and or stylistic element mechanically overused until it becomes an instant cliche.

The other LLM-ism is vapid bullshit: meaningless crap that's kind of like those old hollywood facades: it appears to be a substantial building, but if you look a bit closer it's just a single wall propped up (https://www.ferrovial.com/blog/en/2018/03/the-fake-architect...).
children.textZhHtml:
> 是的，我有过读一本书并观察我认为是克劳德主义的相关经历，结果发现它写于 2019 年。一些常见的故事实际上是很好的写作习惯，但我想它们最好是小剂量的。

法学硕士还没有发明任何东西。每一个法学硕士主义都是一些预先存在的、既定的词语、隐喻和/或文体元素机械地被过度使用，直到它变成即时陈词滥调。

另一个法学硕士主义是乏味的废话：毫无意义的废话，有点像那些旧的好莱坞外墙：它似乎是一座坚固的建筑，但如果你仔细观察，它只是支撑着一堵墙（https://www.ferrovial.com/blog/en/2018/03/the-fake-architect...)。

children.author: SoftTalker
children.textRawHtml:
Sure, that's where the AI got them: the training data. These phrases and cliches were very prevalent especially in corporate "white papers" and memos and marketing materials. There was a time when "stove pipe" was a common one too, along with "silo."

But the LLMs really seem to fixate on using the same ones in the same places all the time. I guess that's because that's the highest probability construction.
children.textZhHtml:
当然，这就是人工智能获取它们的地方：训练数据。这些短语和陈词滥调非常普遍，尤其是在公司“白皮书”、备忘录和营销材料中。曾经有一段时间，“炉管”和“筒仓”一样很常见。

但法学硕士似乎确实一直专注于在相同的地方使用相同的东西。我想这是因为这是概率最高的构造。

children.author: vidarh
children.textRawHtml:
Consider each LLM as one personality. We're getting corporate bullshit from a large number of different personalities, so even when they are similar they are still filtered through many different lenses.

Most of us are only dealing with a handful of hyper-productive LLMs, so it makes sense that the LLM-ticks get old much quicker.
children.textZhHtml:
把每个大语言模型都看作一种独立的人格。我们正在接收来自大量不同人格的企业套话，所以即使它们很相似，也依然是透过许多不同的滤镜过滤出来的。

我们大多数人只接触少数几个高产的大语言模型，所以大语言模型的那些小毛病很快就让人厌烦也就不足为奇了。

children.author: sheept
children.textRawHtml:
Even em dashes have been discouraged by some writers [0] before LLMs because they're easy to overuse.

[0]: https://slate.com/human-interest/2011/05/em-dashes-why-write...
children.textZhHtml:
一些在 LLM 之前的作者 [0] 不鼓励使用破折号，因为它们很容易过度使用。

[0]: https://slate.com/human-interest/2011/05/em-dashes-why-write...

children.author: NoMoreNicksLeft
children.textRawHtml: God help me if Claude starts using inline parentheticals, I might just have to take a vow of (online) silence.
children.textZhHtml: 老天保佑，要是Claude开始用句内括号，我可能就得发誓（在网上）保持沉默了。

children.author: abustamam
children.textRawHtml: Glad to see I'm not the only one who (perhaps) overuses in-line parentheticals... sometimes to the point where I'd have parentheticals within parentheticals!
children.textZhHtml: 很高兴看到我不是唯一一个（也许）过度使用行内括号的人……有时候甚至会用到括号里套括号！

children.author: tzs
children.textRawHtml:
All it takes to get accused of being AI nowadays is to remember and use (1) what you were taught about composition in your high school English class, and (2) what you learned from "The Mac is Not a Typewriter" [1].

[1] https://www.amazon.com/dp/0201782634
children.textZhHtml:
如今，要被指责为人工智能，只需记住并使用 (1) 您在高中英语课上学到的关于作文的知识，以及 (2) 您从“Mac 不是打字机”[1] 中学到的知识。

[1] https://www.amazon.com/dp/0201782634

children.author: mohamedkoubaa
children.textRawHtml: This is exactly why humans invented the idea of things going in and out of style.
children.textZhHtml: 这正是人类发明“事物有流行与过时之分”这一概念的原因。

children.author: _carbyau_
children.textRawHtml: It is one thing to be labelled as "old fashioned" but entirely another to be labelled as an LLM.
children.textZhHtml: 被贴上“老派”的标签是一回事，被贴上“大语言模型”的标签则完全是另一回事。

children.author: nathias
children.textRawHtml:
what do you mean, which tells are good writing practices?

as I see them they are all truly terrible even if authored by humans
children.textZhHtml:
你是什么意思，哪些“特征”算是好的写作习惯？

在我看来，这些特征简直糟糕透顶，哪怕是人类写出来的也一样。

children.author: jambalaya8
children.textRawHtml: Actually, AI was learning these 'AI-isms' even back in 2017/2018 (probably even earlier). I think a lot of people who just jumped on the imaginary AI bandwagon more recently don't realise the mannerisms AIs are adopting are not really new. At some point the bleed between 'you' or 'you' and AI will just become so transparent it will be obliterated, more likely than not.
children.textZhHtml: 事实上，早在2017或2018年（甚至可能更早），AI就已经在学习这些“AI腔调”了。我认为很多最近才盲目跟风炒作AI的人并没有意识到，AI所采用的这些语言习惯其实并不新鲜。在某个时间点，‘你’与AI之间的界限将变得如此透明，以至于很可能会被彻底抹平。

children.author: stingraycharles
children.textRawHtml:
What do you mean? AI is being trained on all data available, so obviously it’s being trained on data from 2017/2018 as well.

Or do you mean that the patterns that AI is showing today were already present in communication around that time?

That seems obvious as well, as AI has a lot of repetitive patterns that come from all kinds of periods, of which “load-bearing” is just one.
children.textZhHtml:
你是什么意思？AI是在所有可用数据上进行训练的，所以显然它也用2017或2018年的数据进行了训练。

还是说你的意思是，AI今天表现出的这些模式在当时那个时期的交流中就已经存在了？

这似乎也是显而易见的，因为AI有很多来自各个时期的重复模式，而“承重”只是其中之一。

children.author: jambalaya8
children.textRawHtml: Yes, I am saying the patterns were emergent already in 2017/2018.
children.textZhHtml: 是的，我想说的是，这些模式在2017/2018年就已经显现出来了。

children.author: schrodinger
children.textRawHtml:
I believe you and OP are in agreement — they were saying that the 2019 book had them, therefore the terms _do_ predate AI. Your point that AI was being trained on material than is load-bearing (lol) but in agreement with OP, not contradictory.

Only mentioning because your "actually" may imply you thought you were disagreeing, when in fact it's one big happy family!
children.textZhHtml:
我认为你和原帖楼主观点一致——他们提到2019年的书里就有这些词，因此这些术语_确实_早于AI出现。你的观点是AI训练所用的材料比承重材料还要多（哈哈），但这与楼主的观点是一致的，并不矛盾。

之所以提这个，是因为你的“其实”可能会让人以为你在反驳，但实际上大家是一家人，其乐融融呢！

children.author: greedo
children.textRawHtml: I wrote a thank you message on Teams to my coworkers on a project, and half of them thought I had used AI to write it. As a professional writer in a previous life, I was astonished. Then they told me that they had never seen me write anything more than a sentence or two so naturally they assumed something relatively polished had to be AI assisted.
children.textZhHtml: 我在 Teams 上给项目同事写了一封感谢信，其中一半人认为我是使用 AI 来写的。作为前世的职业作家，我感到很惊讶。然后他们告诉我，他们从未见过我写过超过一两句话的东西，所以他们很自然地认为相对完善的东西必须由人工智能辅助。

children.author: phs318u
children.textRawHtml: Though I haven’t been a professional writer, I’ve been a good writer with an expansive vocabulary since high school where English was my best subject despite being a STEM maven. I hate the fact that what was previously considered an advantageous skill is now a millstone in public use. I hate having to dumb down and self—censor in order to avoid being accused of using (or being) an LLM. Even though my writing has a few repeated personal tells - certain linguistic errors that I nevertheless employ as part of my idiom (and an LLM never would) - people don’t always notice them. So, I’m forced to change my voice to deal with what’s essentially an IRL Captcha.
children.textZhHtml: 虽然我没当过专业作家，但从高中起我就一直是个文笔不错、词汇量丰富的人，那时英语是我最擅长的科目，尽管我是个理科达人。我痛恨这样一个事实：曾经被视为优势的技能，如今在公共场合却成了累赘。我讨厌为了不被指责使用了（或本身就是）大语言模型，而不得不降低写作水平并进行自我审查。尽管我的文章里有一些反复出现的个人语言特征——某些我作为个人习惯依然会用的语言错误（而大语言模型绝不会这么用）——但人们并不总能注意到这些。所以，我被迫改变自己的文风，去应对这种本质上相当于现实版验证码的荒谬处境。

children.author: leetrout
children.textRawHtml: Serious question: is the em dash misuse a joke?
children.textZhHtml: 认真问一句：破折号误用是个玩笑吗？

comments.author: simonw
comments.textRawHtml: There's some surprising stuff in this codebase. For example, https://github.com/xai-org/grok-build/blob/b189869b7755d2b48... is a "self-contained terminal renderer for Mermaid diagrams", which renders a subset of Mermaid chart types using Unicode box-drawing.
comments.textZhHtml: 这个代码库中有一些令人惊讶的东西。例如，https://github.com/xai-org/grok-build/blob/b189869b7755d2b48... 是“美人鱼图的独立终端渲染器”，它使用 Unicode 方框图来渲染美人鱼图表类型的子集。

children.textRawHtml:
I had Fable 5 compile that Rust code to WebAssembly and build a browser-based playground for it, so you can try it out with Mermaid diagrams here: https://tools.simonwillison.net/grok-mermaid

A few more notes on my Grok code explorations on my blog: https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jul/15/grok-build/
children.textZhHtml:
我让 Fable 5 将 Rust 代码编译为 WebAssembly 并为其构建了一个基于浏览器的 Playground，因此您可以在此处使用美人鱼图进行尝试：https://tools.simonwillison.net/grok-mermaid

在我的博客上还有一些关于我的 Grok 代码探索的注释：https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jul/15/grok-build/

children.author: thrdbndndn
children.textRawHtml:
I love this kind of stuff (ASCII art, if you will), but it just breaks down too easily as soon as Unicode characters (mainly CJK, as I'm Chinese) and fonts are involved.

For example, on your website, any chart or plot involving horizontal arrows breaks down because the assigned font-family (`ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Consolas, monospace`, which ends up as Consolas on my machine) has no such glyph. Thus, it falls back to Segoe UI Symbol, which does not have the same fixed width (or is not fixed-width at all) as other characters: https://i.imgur.com/d2DPGHE.png
children.textZhHtml:
我喜欢这种东西（ASCII 艺术，如果你愿意的话），但一旦涉及 Unicode 字符（主要是 CJK，因为我是中国人）和字体，它就太容易崩溃了。

例如，在您的网站上，任何涉及水平箭头的图表或绘图都会崩溃，因为指定的字体系列（`ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Consolas, monospace`，最终在我的机器上为 Consolas）没有这样的字形。因此，它退回到 Segoe UI 符号，它与其他字符没有相同的固定宽度（或根本不是固定宽度）： https://i.imgur.com/d2DPGHE.png

children.author: uhoh-itsmaciek
children.textRawHtml: I ran into this problem recently on one of our blog posts: we used some Claude output which included tables drawn with Unicode line drawing characters. However, our monospace font did not include these characters, and so rendering fell back to another font in our font stack with different width metrics. I fixed it by using a font that had similar metrics and did include those characters with `unicode-range` (to only select characters we needed) and `size-adjust` (to match font width more exactly), and adding it to the stack. It's a little hacky but works pretty well in practice.
children.textZhHtml: 我最近在一篇博客文章中遇到了这个问题：我们使用了一些 Claude 输出，其中包括用 Unicode 线条画字符绘制的表格。但是，我们的等宽字体不包含这些字符，因此渲染回退到字体堆栈中具有不同宽度度量的另一种字体。我通过使用具有类似指标的字体来修复它，并且确实包含带有 `unicode-range` （仅选择我们需要的字符）和 `size-adjust` （以更准确地匹配字体宽度）的字符，并将其添加到堆栈中。虽然有点 hacky，但在实践中效果很好。

children.author: dolmen
children.textRawHtml: Claude Code with Opus 4.8 is also bad at aligning boxes with content in French (with accentuated letters such as "é" which are multibyte in UTF-8).
children.textZhHtml: 使用 Opus 4.8 的 Claude Code 也不擅长将框与法语内容对齐（带有重音字母，例如“é”，在 UTF-8 中是多字节）。

children.author: dspillett
children.textRawHtml:
> Thus, it falls back to Segoe UI Symbol, which does not have the same fixed width

That seems like a glaring omission to me. If you are rendering fixed-width-per-character text and need to fall back, surely it makes sense to keep to the same character grid even if it does mess up the feel of your negative space somewhat (thin characters having a lot of space around them, wide characters butting into those beside them slightly). You've explicitly asked for text aligned to a grid, either by using a mono-spaced typeface, by using a <pre> tag, or with other relevant CSS choices, the browser should be trying to achieve that.
children.textZhHtml:
> 因此，它会退回到 Segoe UI Symbol，它没有相同的固定宽度

这对我来说似乎是一个明显的遗漏。如果您正在渲染每个字符固定宽度的文本并且需要回退，那么保持相同的字符网格当然是有意义的，即使它确实在某种程度上扰乱了负空间的感觉（细字符周围有很多空间，宽字符稍微撞到了旁边的字符）。您已明确要求文本与网格对齐，无论是通过使用等宽字体、使用 

 标签，还是使用其他相关的 CSS 选择，浏览器应该尝试实现这一点。

children.author: biztos
children.textRawHtml:
Interesting. Thai characters can also blow it out, I imagine because of the difficulty mapping glyphs to width:

https://biztos.com/hey/thai-mermaid-chart.png

To my surprise, Sublime Text gets it almost right:

https://biztos.com/hey/sublime-thai-mermaid.png

I tried finding a Thai monospace font and using that in the HTML but it was worse, probably didn't have the box drawing chars.

Still a fun tool and useful for lots of ASCII cases!
children.textZhHtml:
有趣。泰语字符也可以把它吹出来，我想是因为将字形映射到宽度很困难：

https://biztos.com/hey/thai-mermaid-chart.png

令我惊讶的是，Sublime Text 几乎正确：

https://biztos.com/hey/sublime-thai-mermaid.png

仍然是一个有趣的工具，对许多 ASCII 情况很有用！

children.author: sirn
children.textRawHtml:
The first issue is due to the assumption that character count equals character display width. Thai tone markers usually[1] should not contribute to the display width (เพื่อน is chars = 6, width = 4), so it caused a layout shift.

The second issue is due to the program's layout engine not adjusting the glyph width of a fallback font to that of the main font. A lot of terminals do this, but it's not common for text editors or browsers (arguably this is the correct behavior for non-terminals, since you cannot assume everything must be snapped to a grid).

Fun test for this:

     |กล้วยหอม|
     |Bananas|
This has the same character width. Ghostty, etc., will render it correctly (| aligned). Most browsers and text editors will not.

[1]: some layout engines render free-standing tone markers as 1 character; in that case, this rule only applies to when tone markers are following a character.
children.textZhHtml:
第一个问题是由于字符数等于字符显示宽度的假设造成的。泰语音调标记通常[1] 不应该影响显示宽度（เพื่อน 字符= 6，宽度= 4），因此它导致布局变化。

第二个问题是由于程序的布局引擎没有将后备字体的字形宽度调整为主要字体的字形宽度。很多终端都会这样做，但对于文本编辑器或浏览器来说并不常见（可以说这是非终端的正确行为，因为您不能假设所有内容都必须捕捉到网格）。

对此进行有趣的测试：

 |กล้วยหอม|
     |香蕉|
这具有相同的字符宽度。 Ghostty 等将正确渲染它（| 对齐）。大多数浏览器和文本编辑器不会。

[1]：某些布局引擎将独立的色调标记呈现为 1 个字符；在这种情况下，此规则仅适用于声调标记跟随字符的情况。

children.author: biztos
children.textRawHtml:
Thanks for the explanation.

Safari on iPad lines these up almost perfectly - the second line is a tiny bit wider, I didn’t even notice it at first.

That example had a tone mark but no vowels, so I will try one with both. E&OE.

    |ดื่มน้ำอยู่|
    |abcdef|
[edit] These are even closer, but still imperfectly aligned on my iPad.
children.textZhHtml:
感谢您的解释。

iPad 上的 Safari 几乎完美地排列了这些线 - 第二行稍微宽一点，我一开始甚至没有注意到。

那个示例有声调标记，但没有元音，所以我将尝试同时使用这两个线。 E&OE。

 |ดื่มน้ำอยู่|
    |abcdef|
[编辑] 它们甚至更接近，但在我的 iPad 上仍然不完美对齐。

children.author: numpad0
children.textRawHtml:
This isn't a renderer bug, it's font. Proportional fonts aren't designed with alignment in mind at all, and you can't just expect monospace fonts for all languages outside of ASCII range to be present on random systems, or a single font or font family to support multiple different languages consistently.

You can't really control alignment of deeply Unicode characters like Thai or "→" against monospace characters without serving your own monospace fonts that are guaranteed to work for the characters you'll be sending out, assuming you can always have one in hand.
children.textZhHtml:
这不是渲染器错误，而是字体错误。比例字体在设计时根本没有考虑到对齐，并且您不能期望在随机系统上出现 ASCII 范围之外的所有语言的等宽字体，也不能期望单个字体或字体系列能够一致地支持多种不同的语言。

您无法真正控制深度 Unicode 字符（如泰语或“→”）与等宽字符的对齐，而不提供自己的等宽字体，这些字体保证适用于您要发送的字符，假设您始终可以在其中使用一种等宽字体。手。

children.author: vidarh
children.textRawHtml:
You can still handle this well enough in the renderer, so I'd still consider this a renderer bug - e.g. my terminal emulator will scale any glyph that exceeds the bounding box as defined by the expected number of cells for a given character range. You can't expect any given feature of random characters to align, but I can expect box drawing characters surrounding any given characters to align correctly or consider it a bug (my terminal is almost certainly going to get CJK and Thai wrong - it's entirely untested -, but if so it is a bug, not a font issue).

That includes if I have to fall back, including fallback to proportional fonts, which will look ugly, but work and remain aligned.
children.textZhHtml:
你完全可以在渲染器中妥善处理这个问题，所以我仍然认为这是一个渲染器bug——例如，我的终端模拟器会缩放任何超出给定字符范围预期单元格数量所定义的边界框的字形。你不能指望随机字符的任意特征都能对齐，但我可以期望包围任意字符的制表符能正确对齐，否则就视为bug（我的终端几乎肯定会把中日韩和泰文处理错——因为完全没测试过——但如果是这样，那确实是个bug，而不是字体问题）。

这包括在我不得不回退时，甚至回退到比例字体，虽然看起来会很丑，但能正常工作并保持对齐。

children.author: numpad0
children.textRawHtml: ok... I disagree, and I mean no offense, but there's going to be too much contexts and nuances to be taken into account that it's probably not worth trying for both of us. All I can say is that the problem is not that the pipe characters are given inconsistent width but that non-ASCII characters has all different random widths, and if you need texts with double-width characters like ▶, 漢, ก, etc., to align in a grid, you have to pick a fixed-width(not "monospace") font for the specific language and exclusively use that font for everything within that contiguous text area. Or you can try to fix Unicode so that symbols become variable width so to align to grid or something, but that's going to take a lot of effort.
children.textZhHtml: 好吧……我不同意，无意冒犯，但需要考虑的上下文和细微差别太多了，对我们双方来说可能都不值得去尝试。我只能说，问题不在于管道符的宽度不一致，而在于非ASCII字符的宽度全都不一样且随机，如果你需要让包含▶、漢、ก等全角字符的文本在网格中对齐，你就必须为特定语言选择一种固定宽度（不是“等宽”）字体，并在该连续文本区域内专门使用该字体。或者你可以尝试修改Unicode，让符号变成可变宽度以便对齐网格之类的，但那需要耗费大量精力。

children.author: vidarh
children.textRawHtml:
No, you don't need to pick a fixed width font for that. You will get the best results with one, but rescaling the glyphs works just fine. I've written a font renderer. It's not hard.

In fact my terminal, using said font renderer, rescales glyphs by default because even a lot of "fixed width" fonts are buggy and not truly fixed, and so enforcing the grid alignment and scaling to fit was the easiest way to ensure consistency.

Mixing and matching fonts for full coverage works fine, especially for wide characters.
children.textZhHtml:
不，你不需要为此选择固定宽度字体。用固定宽度字体效果最好，但重新缩放字形也完全行得通。我写过字体渲染器，这并不难。

事实上，我的终端使用上述字体渲染器，默认就会缩放字形，因为即使是很多“固定宽度”字体也有bug，并非真正固定，所以强制网格对齐并缩放以适应是确保一致性的最简单方法。

混合搭配字体以实现全面覆盖效果很好，尤其是对宽字符而言。

children.author: numpad0
children.textRawHtml:
I thought this discussion is more about somehow aligning pipes over multiple lines in existing console emulators(impossible), than about implementing a complete custom graphical text rendering system specific to your app that butcher font files to put glyphs wherever you want?

That feels like cheating since you're not rendering provided font at that point. Besides you might as well just use SVG for diagrams than pretending to be text only.
children.textZhHtml:
我以为这个讨论更多是关于如何在现有控制台模拟器中以某种方式跨多行对齐管道符（这不可能），而不是关于为你的应用实现一个完整的自定义图形文本渲染系统，把字体文件大卸八块以便把字形放在你想要的任何地方？

这感觉像是在作弊，因为那时你根本不是在渲染提供的字体。再说了，你不如直接用SVG来画图，而不是假装只使用纯文本。

children.author: oneeyedpigeon
children.textRawHtml: That test looks pixel perfect in Chrome Android.
children.textZhHtml: 该测试在 Chrome Android 中看起来像素完美。

children.author: torginus
children.textRawHtml: Considering the Chinese are one of the major contributors to AI, I would think this was a solved problem by now, at least in some other CLI based coding agent.
children.textZhHtml: 考虑到中国人是人工智能的主要贡献者之一，我认为这个问题现在已经解决了，至少在其他一些基于 CLI 的编码代理中是这样。

children.author: numpad0
children.textRawHtml: Aren't those non-ASCII "rich" symbols from Japanese fonts around PC-98/Win95 domains anyway? For me with my background, it was always obvious that mixing full-width character in ASCII text never go well for various reasons. For ASCII arts, it is obvious that vertical lines never line up, and there are going to be tons of wasted spaces and different kinds of whitespaces needed to compensate for those. I wonder if specifying MS Gothic and retuning widths for it could help, at least for Windows/Linux.
children.textZhHtml: 这些非 ASCII“丰富”符号不是来自 PC-98/Win95 域周围的日语字体吗？对于我这样的背景来说，很明显，由于各种原因，在 ASCII 文本中混合全角字符永远不会顺利。对于 ASCII 艺术，很明显，垂直线永远不会对齐，并且将有大量浪费的空间和需要不同类型的空白来补偿这些空间。我想知道指定 MS Gothic 并重新调整它的宽度是否有帮助，至少对于 Windows/Linux 来说是这样。

children.author: jack1243star
children.textRawHtml: Funny you'd mention MS Gothic (fixed-width), since old school Japanese ASCII-art on image boards assume MS PGothic (proportional!) instead. Some sites even try to detect ASCII art and force font-family on that specific post.
children.textZhHtml: 有趣的是，您提到了 MS Gothic（固定宽度），因为图像板上的老式日语 ASCII 艺术假定为 MS PGothic（比例！）。有些网站甚至尝试检测 ASCII 艺术并在该特定帖子上强制使用字体系列。

children.author: numpad0
children.textRawHtml:
Yup, 2ch.net/5ch.net/5ch.io uses the MS P Gothic and there were app features and fonts like Mona Font to emulate that. They were not imageboards, though.

I just thought that MS Gothic(non-P) should be kind of widely supported, have all the symbols you need, while also being a monospace, unlike most monospace fonts that only support ASCII symbols.
children.textZhHtml:
是的，2ch.net/5ch.net/5ch.io 使用 MS P Gothic，并且有应用程序功能和字体（例如 Mona Font）来模拟它。不过，它们不是图像板。

我只是认为 MS Gothic（非 P）应该得到广泛支持，拥有您需要的所有符号，同时也是等宽字体，与大多数仅支持 ASCII 符号的等宽字体不同。

children.author: pmarreck
children.textRawHtml: I was going to say, perhaps generate a failing test case, but testing for proper unicode rendering might be tricky??
children.textZhHtml: 我想说，也许生成一个失败的测试用例，但是测试正确的 unicode 渲染可能很棘手？

children.author: ronbenton
children.textRawHtml: IMO this is the social internet at its best. Pretty obscure question answered relatively quickly with answer and source.
children.textZhHtml: 我认为这是最好的社交互联网。相当晦涩的问题相对较快地得到了答案和来源。

children.author: Waterluvian
children.textRawHtml:
AI immediately gives me the same answer. I can’t tell if I like this easy access to detail or lament the growing irrelevance of “social internet” for these kinds of things.

It reminds me of pre-phone disagreements among pals. You’d argue and argue and maybe eventually agree to disagree. Today someone just looks up the trivia and it’s all over.
children.textZhHtml:
人工智能立即给了我同样的答案。我不知道我是否喜欢这种轻松获取细节的方式，还是感叹“社交互联网”对此类事情越来越不相关。

这让我想起了朋友之间电话前的分歧。你们会争论再争论，也许最终会同意不同意。今天有人随便查一下琐事就完事了。

children.author: pimlottc
children.textRawHtml: Of course, the only reason AI knows about it is because one human posted it online because they wanted to share a good story with another human
children.textZhHtml: 当然，人工智能知道它的唯一原因是一个人将其发布到网上，因为他们想与另一个人分享一个好故事

children.author: justusthane
children.textRawHtml: I really worry about AI leading to the information death of the internet. If everyone is getting their info from AI, rather than actually reading websites/blogs/forums, is there any incentive for posting things like this on the internet in the first place?
children.textZhHtml: 我真的很担心人工智能会导致互联网的信息死亡。如果每个人都从人工智能获取信息，而不是实际阅读网站/博客/论坛，那么首先是否有动力在互联网上发布此类内容？

children.author: bdcravens
children.textRawHtml: I don't know if I'm unique in this, but I find myself asking people I know about things that I know I can easily lookup faster. At this point it's more a social ritual than actual information gathering.
children.textZhHtml: 我不知道我在这方面是否独一无二，但我发现自己向我认识的人询问我知道我可以轻松更快地查找的事情。在这一点上，这更像是一种社交仪式，而不是实际的信息收集。

children.author: order-matters
children.textRawHtml:
same here, but only when it feels like good conversation or is a subject we already discuss. Especially if youre in the same room, then it can become a little trivia game and if no one knows then someone can look it up

related note, my girlfriend is bilingual with spanish, and i only have some old high school classes of spanish to go off of, so whenever she texts me a word i dont recognize i ask her what it means. Aside from helping me understand, she gets a peek into my literacy level (which is admittedly pretty low), i can call out the word when its used again, and i get the impression she likes teaching me these little things.

extending the lesson beyond our little ritual, when you ask another person for the information it goes beyond being useful to each other. it is a bid for connection, and a display that you trust them and their opinion/knowledge on the subject.
children.textZhHtml:
这里也一样，但前提是感觉像是很好的对话或者是我们已经讨论过的主题。 特别是如果你们在同一个房间，那么它可能会变成一个小问答游戏，如果没有人知道，那么有人可以查一下

相关注释，我的女朋友会说西班牙语，而我只有一些旧的高中西班牙语课要上，所以每当她给我发短信时，我不认识一个单词，我会问她这意味着什么。 除了帮助我理解之外，她还了解了我的识字水平（诚然，我的识字水平相当低），当我再次使用这个词时，我可以喊出这个词，而且我觉得她喜欢教我这些小事情。

将课程扩展到我们的小仪式之外，当你向另一个人询问信息时，它不仅仅是对彼此有用。 这是一种建立联系的方式，并表明您信任他们以及他们对该主题的看法/知识。

children.author: seedboot
children.textRawHtml: It's just like we evolved to be social creatures!
children.textZhHtml: 这就像我们进化成为社交生物一样！

children.author: MisterTea
children.textRawHtml:
> AI immediately gives me the same answer.

"The search engine found the answer too" is not an interesting response.
children.textZhHtml:
> AI 立即给了我同样的答案。

“搜索引擎也找到了答案”并不是一个有趣的回答。

children.author: dylan604
children.textRawHtml: In the early days of the smart phone, I had heard it referred to as the Bar Bet Settler 5000. It was pulled out of one's pocket and with its web browser one would use Google's search page to find information to settle the bet. Then, those smart phones got infected with social media apps and the Bar Bet Settler 5000 went the way of the dodo.
children.textZhHtml: 在智能手机的早期，我听说它被称为 Bar Bet Settler 5000。它是从口袋里拿出来的，通过它的网络浏览器，人们可以使用 Google 的搜索页面来查找信息来结算赌注。然后，这些智能手机感染了社交媒体应用程序，Bar Bet Settler 5000 也步了渡渡鸟的后尘。

children.author: Forgeties79
children.textRawHtml: As more people offload their search/mental effort to LLM’s and fewer people take the time to answer these obscure questions, unfortunately we will simply lose the fun portion while making LLM’s incapable of answering them. So good news is you don’t have to make a decision!
children.textZhHtml: 随着越来越多的人将他们的搜索/脑力工作转移给法学硕士，而花时间回答这些晦涩的问题的人越来越少，不幸的是，我们只会失去其中的乐趣，同时使法学硕士无法回答这些问题。好消息是您不必做出决定！

comments.author: kalleboo
comments.textRawHtml:
> Some code associated with Nedryland is visible on screen. It looks like actual source code[9] with Classic Mac OS API functions calls

The source code shown is example code included with the Macintosh Programmers Workshop, Apple's original IDE for the Mac. Originally sold as a separate product, eventually it was provided on the Developer CDs and then as a free online download as serious developers had moved to CodeWarrior. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Macintosh_Programmer's_Worksho...

One of the windows shows the example for how to make a HyperCard XCMD and the other one looks like an MPW script for using Apple's Projector source control.

edit: Found the files in question in a copy of MPW 3.1. Line endings have been converted from CR to LF and the character set from MacOS Roman to UTF-8 to display easily in modern browsers

MPW 3.1:Examples:HyperXExamples:Reduce.p https://kalleboo.com/linked/Reduce.p.txt

MPW 3.1:Examples:Examples:CheckOutActive https://kalleboo.com/linked/CheckOutActive.txt

MPW 3.1:Examples:Examples:DerezPict https://kalleboo.com/linked/DerezPict.txt
comments.textZhHtml:
> 一些与 Nedryland 相关的代码在屏幕上可见。它看起来像带有经典 Mac OS API 函数调用的实际源代码[9]

显示的源代码是 Macintosh Programmers Workshop（Apple 最初的 Mac IDE）中包含的示例代码。最初作为单独的产品出售，最终它以开发人员 CD 的形式提供，然后随着认真的开发人员转向 CodeWarrior，作为免费的在线下载。 https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Macintosh_Programmer's_Worksho...

其中一个窗口显示了如何制作 HyperCard XCMD 的示例，另一个窗口看起来像使用 Apple 投影仪源代码控制的 MPW 脚本。

comments.author: pivo
comments.textRawHtml:
My wife worked for Thinking Machines back then. I remember that they'd asked Cray to loan them a supercomputer for the film because that's the computer used in the book. Cray brushed them off, so they turned to Thinking Machines who were happy to do it.

To thank them, the producers rented a theater in Cambridge, MA to screen the film just for Thinking Machines and I was also able to attend. By far the biggest reactions from the audience that night were when the CM-5 was shown for the first time and then when the young actress says, "It's a Unix system. I know this"
comments.textZhHtml:
我的妻子当时在 Thinking Machines 工作。我记得他们要求克雷借给他们一台用于电影的超级计算机，因为这就是书中使用的计算机。克雷拒绝了他们，所以他们转向 Thinking Machines，他们很乐意这样做。

为了感谢他们，制片人在马萨诸塞州剑桥租了一个剧院专门为 Thinking Machines 放映这部电影，我也能参加。到目前为止，当晚观众最大的反应是当 CM-5 第一次展示时，然后当年轻女演员说：“这是一个 Unix 系统。我知道这一点”

children.author: dhosek
children.textRawHtml: “It’s a Unix system. I know this.” was definitely the line from the movie most quoted in online spaces in the 90s.
children.textZhHtml: “这是一个 Unix 系统。我知道这一点。”这绝对是 90 年代网络空间中引用次数最多的电影台词。

children.author: kraquepype
children.textRawHtml:
I wonder how many sysadmin or adjacent careers that scene helped to foster.

As a kid JP was a favorite of mine, and eventually I got into Linux as an older teen.

That sequence cemented Unix as this mythical thing in my mind and I knew I had to know more. I eventually got to work on AIX in my first sysadmin role which was great. I leaned more into Linux, but it had a positive effect on my IT career.
children.textZhHtml:
我想知道这个场景帮助培养了多少系统管理员或相关职业。

小时候 JP 是我的最爱，最终我在十几岁的时候进入了 Linux。

这个序列巩固了 Unix 在我心目中的神话般的地位，我知道我必须了解更多。我最终开始在 AIX 上工作，担任我的第一个系统管理员角色，这很棒。我更倾向于 Linux，但这对我的 IT 职业生涯产生了积极的影响。

stories.title: Are we offloading too much of our thinking to AI?
stories.titleZh: 我们是否把太多的思考都外包给了人工智能？

comments.author: zerobees
comments.textRawHtml:
I don't know if this is a good framing. "Too much" is subjective, and every heavy AI user will assert that they're just unlocking their potential, that calculators didn't make us dumber, etc.

But to latch onto the calculator argument: if you outsource adding numbers to a calculator, you're still you. On the flip side, if you use an LLM do most of your thinking, what's left? We have people here who use LLMs to raise their children, to manage relationships, to design products. So what's your unique contribution to this world - is it the prompt you once wrote? You're standing in front of a token-generating machine, pulling a lever, sometimes receiving gifts. Is that your edge, your unique experience, your purpose in life?

Many LLM maximalists say they use the tech to learn new things, but to what effect? Are you going to apply that knowledge of physics or computer science yourself, or will you just prompt the LLM again?

In my mind, it's pretty simple: I'm a human, LLMs are not. If a human writes a novel, it's inherently worth more because it's hard-earned and anchored to experiences we share. I want to support that. And I want to be a human who can write novels, the old-fashioned way. I'm not good at lifting weights or running, so my thinking is the only thing I have.
comments.textZhHtml:
我不知道这是否是一个好的框架。 “太多”是主观的，每个人工智能重度用户都会断言，他们只是在释放自己的潜力，计算器并没有让我们变得更笨，等等。

但要抓住计算器的论点：如果你将添加数字的工作外包给计算器，你仍然是你。另一方面，如果你使用法学硕士来完成你的大部分思考，还剩下什么？我们这里有人利用法学硕士来抚养孩子、管理人际关系、设计产品。那么你对这个世界的独特贡献是什么——是你曾经写过的提示吗？你站在代币生成机前，拉动杠杆，有时会收到礼物。这是你的优势、你独特的经历、你的人生目标吗？

许多法学硕士最大化主义者说他们使用技术来学习新事物，但效果如何呢？你会自己应用物理或计算机科学的知识，还是会再次提示法学硕士？

在我看来，这很简单：我是人，法学硕士不是。如果一个人写了一本小说，它本质上就更有价值，因为它来之不易，并且植根于我们分享的经历。我想支持这一点。我想成为一个可以用老式方式写小说的人。我不擅长举重或跑步，所以我的思考是我唯一的东西。

children.author: jvanderbot
children.textRawHtml:
Have you read the "Whispering earring" essay? I love it for the LLM era.[1]

You can treat AI as a whispering earring - "What should we do now? How do we fix this? What do you think?" Or you can treat it like an exoskelton - "Implement kd-tree with metric space xyz for this problem, mapping this to that blah blah".

That's pre-thought execution automation that makes review much simpler - you already know the shape of the desired output. The whispering earring is atrophy.

1. https://croissanthology.com/earring
children.textZhHtml:
你读过《耳语耳环》这篇文章吗？我喜欢 LLM 时代。[1]

你可以将人工智能视为耳语耳环 - “我们现在应该做什么？我们如何解决这个问题？你觉得怎么样？”或者你可以把它当作一个 exoskelton - “针对这个问题使用度量空间 xyz 实现 kd 树，将其映射到那个 blah blah”。

这是预先考虑的执行自动化，使审查变得更加简单 - 你已经知道所需输出的形状。耳语耳环萎缩了。

1. https://croissanthology.com/earring

children.author: zerobees
children.textRawHtml: It cracks me up that you bring up the exoskeleton metaphor, because I'm pretty sure it originated from a 100% AI-generated essay that made it to the top of HN a while back. So I guess, AI is whispering things into our ears whether we notice or not.
children.textZhHtml: 你提到外骨骼的比喻让我很高兴，因为我很确定它源自一篇 100% AI 生成的文章，不久前它登上了 HN 的榜首。所以我猜，无论我们是否注意到，人工智能都在向我们耳语。

children.author: quietbritishjim
children.textRawHtml: Exoskeleton is such an obvious metaphor for AI (at least, one potential ways of using it) that surely no one, human or AI, could possibly claim credit for it.
children.textZhHtml: 外骨骼是人工智能的一个明显的隐喻（至少是使用它的一种潜在方式），肯定没有人，无论是人类还是人工智能，都可以声称它是它的功劳。

children.author: garciasn
children.textRawHtml: I had been explaining it to others as 'My Iron Man suit' before reading the exoskeleton essay, months later. So, you are definitely correct.
children.textZhHtml: 几个月后，在阅读那篇外骨骼文章之前，我一直在向其他人解释它是“我的钢铁侠套装”。所以，你绝对是正确的。

children.author: dTal
children.textRawHtml: It feels more like a BMX bike to me. The quality of what I get out seems to be a direct function of what I put in. The threshold of what I can achieve is raised slightly, the speed with which I can achieve it is raised quite a lot - but it's not a magic rocket ship that whisks me to far off hostile environments and protects me while I'm there. I'm still "naked". The code is still mine, and I can get off the bike at any point and walk.
children.textZhHtml: 对我来说，它更像是一辆小轮车。我得到的产出质量似乎与我投入的精力成正比。它能让我达到的上限稍微提高了一点，达到目标的速度也大大加快了——但它不是一艘神奇的火箭飞船，能把我瞬间送到遥远恶劣的环境中并保护我。我依然是“赤身裸体”的。代码依然是我的，我随时可以下车步行。

children.author: kakacik
children.textRawHtml:
Thats... not how most of the world is using llms. Students are getting measurably dumber (as in lacking actual topic understanding, since actually learning sonething properly is much harder than shallow knowledge), every other day I read about somebody losing their wealth due to listening to llms with finances.

Other recent articles - psychologists start seeing people losing their uniqueness and many people slowly veering towards same middle bland opinions.

I think top parent is correct - we are in the process of visibly losing a lot from our humanity, much more than we are gaining. We shouldnt be listening too much to loudest mouths here that praise efficiency gains, many of those are 'semi-autistic' folks with tunnel vision focused on 1 aspect and ignoring larger picture.

I dont see any change of course possible, the sweet nectar of efficiency and offloading decisions and work is too strong a drug to ignore. But everything has its set of benefits and drawbacks, and drawbacks here seem pretty brutal so far.
children.textZhHtml:
这……可不是世界上大多数人使用大语言模型的方式。学生们明显变笨了（指的是缺乏对主题的真正理解，因为真正学好一样东西比获取肤浅的知识要难得多），我隔三差五就能看到有人因为听信大语言模型的理财建议而破财的新闻。

最近的其他文章——心理学家开始发现人们正在失去独特性，许多人正慢慢转向千篇一律、平庸乏味的观点。

我认为最高赞评论说得对——我们正在明显失去很多人性中的东西，失去的远比得到的多。我们不应该太听信这里那些吹捧效率提升的最大嗓门，他们中很多人是那种“半自闭症”患者，视野狭窄，只盯着一个方面而忽视全局。

我看不到任何改变航向的可能，效率和将决策与工作外包带来的甜蜜诱惑就像一剂猛药，让人无法抗拒。但凡事都有利弊，而目前的弊端看起来相当残酷。

children.author: NoMoreNicksLeft
children.textRawHtml:
>So I guess, AI is whispering things into our ears whether we notice or not.

Humanity is a weak hive mind species. You can, with effort, keep it from whispering it into your ears, if you really want that. But it's impossible to keep it from whispering in another ear attached to your hivemind. And the only difference in those ears is the propagation delay before it's rattling around in your own skull.
children.textZhHtml:
>所以我猜，无论我们是否注意到，人工智能都在向我们耳语。

人类是一种弱小的蜂巢思维物种。如果你真的想要的话，你可以通过努力阻止它在你耳中低语。但要阻止它在你的蜂巢思维的另一只耳朵里低语是不可能的。这些耳朵的唯一区别是它在您自己的头骨中发出嘎嘎声之前的传播延迟。

children.author: paytonjjones
children.textRawHtml:
Something I never considered much is what happens when everyone else is using the Whispering Earring.

You may be more free and independent, but you may also be unable to compete as everyone else easily gains wealth and success. Natural selection doesn't particularly care about freedom of consciousness.

Bleak.
children.textZhHtml:
我从未过多考虑过，当其他所有人都在使用低语耳环时会发生什么。

你或许会更加自由和独立，但也可能因为其他所有人都能轻易获取财富与成功而无法与之竞争。自然选择并不怎么在乎意识的自由。

令人绝望。

children.author: ben_w
children.textRawHtml:
Eventually, that becomes interesting. Before we get to that stage, I think we pass through (almost) everyone following almost the exact same advice as each other.

Such people are extremely predictable.

You may have already noticed, before LLMs, cliché? Talking points that make a group identifiable? Words and phrases that act as applause lights or cognitive stop signs? (That last sentence itself being a pair of clichés that you can use to identify where I hang out online).

Anyway, point is, LLMs will give us a memetic monoculture before they turn us all into a world of correctly personalised Whispering Earring wearers. That makes them predictable, that makes them exploitable. It'll be like playing chess against someone you know is using a specific version of Stockfish: even though it would beat you if you tried to fight the system unaided, you can win by asking your own copy of the AI to go one step further ahead, and it will be accurate precisely because it's playing against itself and reacting to its own moves.

(Of course, the fact I've said this in writing means this is in the training data; in the general case this means the LLMs will know that and account for that, but I suspect comments like this won't shift the needle all that much compared to the aggregate output of 3 billion people reacting to short-form emotional manipulation A/B slop)
children.textZhHtml:
最终，这会变得很有趣。但在达到那个阶段之前，我认为我们会经历一个过程：（几乎）每个人都在遵循几乎完全相同的建议。

这样的人是极其可预测的。

你可能已经注意到了，在LLM出现之前，那些陈词滥调？能让某个群体被识别出来的谈资？那些起到“掌声灯”或“认知停止标志”作用的词句？（这最后一句话本身就是两个陈词滥调，你可以借此认出我在哪个网络圈子混）。

总之，重点是，LLM会先给我们带来一个模因单一种植的世界，然后再把我们全都变成戴着完美个性化“低语耳环”的人。这让他们变得可预测，也变得可被利用。这就像和你知道正在使用特定版本Stockfish的人下棋：尽管如果你试图单枪匹马对抗系统，它肯定会打败你，但你可以让自己的AI副本多往前算一步从而获胜，而且它之所以准确，正是因为它在和自己对弈，并对自己的走法做出反应。

（当然，我把这些写成文字意味着这已经进入了训练数据；在一般情况下，这意味着LLM会知道这一点并将其考虑在内，但我怀疑，与30亿人对短平快情感操纵A/B测试垃圾内容做出的总体反应相比，像这样的评论不会让指针偏移太多）

children.author: csh0
children.textRawHtml:
This is already underway.

My dog often gets misidentified as a restricted breed. This used to make apartment hunting difficult because, occasionally, the property manager would visually ID the dog breed as banned, I’d have to go to the vet and get paperwork, potentially gene testing, arguing she wasn't, it was a whole thing.

But, recently, the apartment I moved into had an online portal where I had to upload a photo and it would identify the breed to determine if it was approved.

I correctly assumed the portal was using an LLM for this purpose. I wrote a script which submitted different photos of my dog to the major LLM providers until it found a photo which all the LLMs would identify as the correct breed.

I simply submitted that photo and, as expected, passed with flying colors.
children.textZhHtml:
这已经在进行了。

我的狗经常被误认为是禁养品种。这以前让我找公寓很头疼，因为偶尔物业经理会凭肉眼认定狗是禁养品种，我就得去兽医那儿开证明，甚至可能要做基因检测，来证明它不是，这简直折腾死人。

但最近，我搬进的公寓有一个在线门户，我必须上传一张照片，它会识别品种以确定是否被批准。

我准确地假设该门户使用LLM来实现这一目的。我写了一个脚本，将我的狗的不同照片提交给各大LLM提供商，直到找到一张所有LLM都能识别为正确品种的照片。

我直接提交了那张照片，不出所料，顺利通过了。

children.author: ForOldHack
children.textRawHtml: I was anticipating that it would have reversed course and flagged you. Blessings on you, and your four footed friend.
children.textZhHtml: 我本以为它会改变方向并标记你。祝福你，还有你的四足小伙伴。

stories.title: European "age verification" "app" forcing everyone to use Android or iOS
stories.titleZh: 欧洲“年龄验证”“应用”强制所有人使用Android或iOS

comments.author: blop
comments.textRawHtml:
This is the elephant in the room regarding the big "digital sovereignty" talks in the EU. For the moment in the EU institutions the focus is mostly at the post-acceptance stage that everything must eventually migrate off US clouds. There is still some denial and hope that things will go back to "before" because it's going to be extremely costly to migrate, but at least high level EU civil servants start to see the strategic value of moving out.

However there is ZERO talk about mobile platforms... No alternative solution like linux for the desktop, no money or care given to the few alternative that tentatively exist, and zero talk about forcing companies (at least for the ones shipping android phones) to open up their firmwares and allow users to install alternative OS if they want to sell in the EU.

So whilst the backend guys more or less got the memo about sovereignty, I think there is still a lot of educational work to do regarding end user devices and what kind of digital slavery hole we're digging ourselves in...
comments.textZhHtml:
这就是欧盟大谈“数字主权”时房间里的大象。目前，欧盟机构的关注点主要集中在接受后的阶段，即一切最终都必须从美国云端迁移出去。人们仍心存否认和幻想，希望一切能回到“从前”，因为迁移成本将极其高昂，但至少欧盟高级公务员开始意识到迁出的战略价值。

然而，关于移动平台的讨论却完全为零……没有像桌面端Linux那样的替代方案，对少数初步存在的替代方案既不给资金也不予关注，也完全没有讨论强制企业（至少是那些销售安卓手机的企业）开放固件，并允许用户如果想进入欧盟市场销售就必须安装替代操作系统。

因此，虽然后端人员或多或少已经领会了关于主权的指示，但我认为在终端用户设备以及我们正在为自己挖掘何种数字奴役陷阱方面，仍有大量教育工作要做……

children.author: zoobab
children.textRawHtml:
"zero talk about forcing companies (at least for the ones shipping android phones) to open up their firmwares and allow users to install alternative OS if they want to sell in the EU."

Complete public datasheets on how to program the hardware should be a requirement fit a DMA2.0.
children.textZhHtml:
“完全没有讨论强制企业（至少是那些销售安卓手机的企业）开放固件，并允许用户如果想进入欧盟市场销售就必须安装替代操作系统。”

关于如何对硬件进行编程的完整公开数据表应成为DMA 2.0的一项要求。

children.author: omnimus
children.textRawHtml:
This is not entirely true. I don't have much details but I know people who started to work on two separate free software projects aiming to make supported mobile OS.
These projects couldn't get funding before but they do now. Afaik it's still a battle with AI companies lobbying that soverign AI is much more important than mobile OS but there is some growing interest.
Imho i don't even think some linux based alternative to Android would be that hard to pull off but it's the hw companies that will be skeptical to build hw for such OS. I would have to be some govs puahing it as secure gov devices first.
children.textZhHtml: 这并不完全正确。我没有太多细节，但我认识一些人，他们已经开始着手两个独立的自由软件项目，旨在打造受支持的移动操作系统。这些项目以前拿不到资金，但现在可以了。据我所知，目前仍在与人工智能公司进行博弈，这些公司游说称主权AI比移动操作系统重要得多，但也出现了一些日益增长的兴趣。在我看来，我甚至不认为基于Linux的安卓替代方案会有多难实现，难的是硬件公司会对为这种操作系统制造硬件持怀疑态度。这必须首先由一些政府将其作为安全的政府设备来推动。

children.author: AnthonyMouse
children.textRawHtml: Which is all well and good, but who is going to use those projects if the law requires them to use Android or iOS for age verification?
children.textZhHtml: 这听起来都很好，但如果法律要求他们使用Android或iOS进行年龄验证，谁还会去用那些项目呢？

children.author: izacus
children.textRawHtml:
"The law" requires no such thing and there's nothing preventing those new OSes form providing proper security signals.

But is DOES require work - and it's much easier to complain than to put in work.
children.textZhHtml:
“法律”并没有这样的要求，也没有什么能阻止这些新操作系统提供适当的安全信号。

但这确实需要付出努力——而抱怨总是比付诸行动容易得多。

children.author: AnthonyMouse
children.textRawHtml:
> "The law" requires no such thing

It requires age verification and provides code whose development was subsidized by the government, which third parties the user doesn't control will use, that creates a dependency on those platforms.

> there's nothing preventing those new OSes form providing proper security signals.

A network effect, far from being nothing, is a barrier the height of a mountain.

The purpose of attestation is to lock out competing platforms. It security value is a joke. Devices pass attestation with known vulnerabilities and fail it for being competitors, even if the competitors have better security.

Offering to make attestations nobody accepts is a farce. The problem to be solved is how to run existing software that was originally written for other platforms when the new platform is new and doesn't have enough users for third party developers to specifically target it, which is the exact thing that can't do. And without that it can't get enough users for third party developers to specifically target it.
children.textZhHtml:
> “法律”并没有这样的要求

它要求进行年龄验证，并提供了由政府补贴开发的代码，用户无法控制的第三方将使用这些代码，这就产生了对那些平台的依赖。

> 没有任何东西能阻止这些新操作系统提供适当的安全信号。

网络效应绝非无足轻重，它是一道如山般高耸的壁垒。

认证的目的就是把竞争平台拒之门外。它的安全价值简直是个笑话。设备带着已知的漏洞也能通过认证，却仅仅因为是竞争对手就被拒之门外，哪怕竞争对手的安全性能更好。

提供没人接受的认证简直是一场闹剧。需要解决的问题是，当新平台刚起步、没有足够的用户让第三方开发者专门为其开发时，如何运行最初为其他平台编写的现有软件，而这恰恰是做不到的。没有这一点，它就无法获得足够的用户来让第三方开发者专门为其开发。

children.author: izacus
children.textRawHtml:
> The purpose of attestation is to lock out competing platforms. It security value is a joke.

This is kind of your... opinion man.

In reality pretty much all security sensitive applications require attestation from their side.
children.textZhHtml:
> 认证的目的是为了排挤竞争平台。它的安全价值简直是个笑话。

这多少只是你……个人的看法，老兄。

实际上，几乎所有安全敏感型应用都需要他们那边提供认证。

children.author: AnthonyMouse
children.textRawHtml:
And what security value does that provide, when millions of attestation-passing devices have public unpatched LPE vulnerabilities? Anyone can get one and run arbitrary code on it as root. It's completely worthless for actual security. Worse, it does the opposite, because a newer third party ROM that patches those vulnerabilities would fail attestation, preventing honest users from updating their device and thereby leaving them vulnerable.

What it does do is require you to get one of those devices instead of a competing device or OS, thereby locking out competitors but not attackers.
children.textZhHtml:
当数百万台通过认证的设备存在公开的未修复本地权限提升漏洞时，这能提供什么安全价值呢？任何人都能弄到一台，并以root身份在上面运行任意代码。这对实际安全来说毫无价值。更糟的是，它起到了相反的作用，因为一个修复了这些漏洞的较新第三方ROM将无法通过认证，从而阻止了诚实用户更新设备，使他们继续暴露在风险之中。

它真正做的只是要求你购买这些设备中的一台，而不是竞争对手的设备或操作系统，从而将竞争对手拒之门外，却挡不住攻击者。

children.author: roundabout-host
children.textRawHtml: It requires a government-authorised "age verification" "app", but it does not require that it is accessible through standard protocols, so that it can work on any platform. In practice, the governments will only make it available for Android and iOS. Plus, you cannot have a free OS providing "attestation"; "attestation" is incompatible with root access and modifying the OS.
children.textZhHtml: 它要求使用政府授权的“年龄验证”“应用”，但并没有要求它必须通过标准协议访问以便在任何平台上运行。实际上，政府只会让它在Android和iOS上可用。此外，自由操作系统无法提供“认证”；“认证”与root权限和修改操作系统是不兼容的。

children.author: hnisnotbenign
children.textRawHtml: Yes, it is much easier to ignore the unelected bureaucrats than to jump through their ridiculous hoops.
children.textZhHtml: 是的，无视那些未经选举产生的官僚，比去应付他们那些荒谬的繁文缛节要容易得多。

children.author: UqWBcuFx6NV4r
children.textRawHtml:
See, you’re just saying what they’re saying, but with emotive, thought-terminating language. Again, it’s easier to complain. Have you been living under a rock for your entire life? Have never ever been involved in a decision being made about certain “blessed” vendors, and the decision is being made for legitimate technical reasons, not “ridiculous” ones.

If you’re the sort of person that’s unable to distinguish between something that’s legitimately unjustifiable/ridiculous, and something that just upsets you, then you do you, but don’t bring this here pretending that it suffices as a discussion, because it doesn’t.
children.textZhHtml:
你看，你只是在重复他们的话，只不过用了些煽情、终止思考的套话。再说一遍，抱怨总是更容易的。你这辈子是一直躲在石头缝里吗？难道你从来没有参与过关于某些“钦定”供应商的决策，而且这个决策是出于合理的技术原因，而不是什么“荒谬”的原因吗？

如果你是那种无法区分真正站不住脚或荒谬的事情与仅仅是让你不爽的事情的人，那你就自便吧，但别把这带到这儿来，还假装这算是一场讨论，因为这根本不算。

children.author: hnisnotbenign
children.textRawHtml: What?
children.textZhHtml: 什么？

children.author: omnimus
children.textRawHtml: I think the asumption being if there is gov backed mobile OS govs would also support their app ecosystem there. That's kinda the point of funding alternative free mobile OS?
children.textZhHtml: 我认为假设是，如果有政府支持的移动操作系统，政府也会在那里支持他们的应用生态系统。这大概就是资助替代性免费移动操作系统的意义所在吧？

children.author: mghackerlady
children.textRawHtml: They could try and put money into funding Jolla/sailfish/whatever
children.textZhHtml: 他们可以试着出资资助Jolla/Sailfish或其他什么项目

children.author: tokai
children.textRawHtml: That wont help anything. Then you are just force to use Android, iOS, or Sailfish. It need to be a platform agnostic thing, else you're just hitching the fulcrum of civil society on private company.
children.textZhHtml: 那无济于事。那样你只是被迫使用Android、iOS或Sailfish。这必须是一个平台无关的事情，否则你只是把公民社会的支点绑在了私营公司身上。

children.author: roundabout-host
children.textRawHtml: Exactly. The app is only inclusive if it is accessible over a standard protocol (Web) without "attestation", so that it works for any platform. If I release a GNU/Linux distribution tomorrow, I should be able to use the app on it with only some work on my part.
children.textZhHtml: 没错。只有当应用通过标准协议（Web）访问且无需“认证”时，它才是真正包容的，这样才能在任何平台上运行。如果我明天发布一个GNU/Linux发行版，我应该只需做一点适配工作就能在上面使用这个应用。

children.author: mghackerlady
children.textRawHtml: I don't disagree, but the eu needs their own mobile OS alternative in general so if they absolutely need to rely on a private company, it isn't an american one
children.textZhHtml: 我不反对，但欧盟总体上需要有自己的移动操作系统替代方案，所以如果他们绝对需要依赖一家私营公司，那也不该是美国公司。

children.textRawHtml: This doesn't follow. Why is public infrastructure no longer a possibility as soon as computers get involved? We aren't talking about cutting edge innovation here anymore, mobile phones are boring standard devices.
children.textZhHtml: 这说不通。为什么一涉及到计算机，公共基础设施就不再可能了？我们在这里谈论的不再是前沿创新，手机已经是无聊的标准设备了。

children.author: mghackerlady
children.textRawHtml: It is a possibility, just not one I see as likely to happen. Could the EU fund a public phone company? Probably. Will it happen? Most likely not
children.textZhHtml: 这是一种可能性，只是我不认为它会发生。欧盟能资助一家公共电信公司吗？可能吧。但这会发生吗？大概率不会。

stories.title: Bonsai 27B: A 27B-Class model that runs on a phone
stories.titleZh: Bonsai 27B：一款可在手机上运行的27B级模型

comments.author: SwellJoe
comments.textRawHtml: What I most want to see it compared to is Gemma 4 12B in the 4-bit QAT version. It's barely bigger than this at just under 7GB, so it also runs on just about any modern device and is remarkably smart for its size. It's an excellent tool user, crazy good vision for its size. I'm still trying to wrap my head around how much is lost with each step down in resolution, but the QAT versions from Google seem to prove the answer is "very little" at four bits.
comments.textZhHtml: 我最想看到它与 4 位 QAT 版本的 Gemma 4 12B 进行比较。它的容量略小于 7GB，因此几乎可以在任何现代设备上运行，并且就其尺寸而言非常智能。它是一个优秀的工具用户，对于它的尺寸来说有着非常好的视野。我仍在试图弄清楚分辨率每降低一步会损失多少，但 Google 的 QAT 版本似乎证明答案在四位上“非常少”。

children.author: janalsncm
children.textRawHtml: Based on their numbers and cross referencing with the Gemma numbers, this model crushes Gemma 4 12b on math and coding, is slightly worse on knowledge and tool calling, and is significantly worse on vision tasks.
children.textZhHtml: 根据它们的数据以及与 Gemma 数据的交叉引用，该模型在数学和编码方面碾压 Gemma 4 12b，在知识和工具调用方面稍差，在视觉任务方面明显较差。

children.author: recursivegirth
children.textRawHtml: I think this is where leveraging classifier models will become important. The frontier LLM models do "everything", while we've known for a while that to truly scale this we will need to distill models into their individual functions. I don't see this as necessarily a bad thing and hope more is done in this space. Very promising.
children.textZhHtml: 我认为这就是利用分类器模型变得重要的地方。前沿的法学硕士模型可以做“一切”，而我们已经知道要真正扩展这一点，我们需要将模型提炼成各自的功能。我不认为这一定是一件坏事，并希望在这个领域做更多的事情。非常有前途。

children.author: bigmadshoe
children.textRawHtml: Bitter lesson is knocking. Mixture of experts is essentially what you’re describing but free from unnecessary inductive biases.
children.textZhHtml: 惨痛的教训正在敲响。专家的混合本质上就是你所描述的，但没有不必要的归纳偏见。

children.author: hovering_nox
children.textRawHtml: Mixture of Experts is absolutely not what they're describing. MoE has to be one of the most misleadingly named things ever. It's completely confusing as to what it actually is.
children.textZhHtml: 混合专家模型绝对不是他们描述的那样。MoE绝对是有史以来名字最具误导性的概念之一。它到底是什么，完全让人一头雾水。

children.author: bigmadshoe
children.textRawHtml: Note the “but free from unnecessary inductive biases” part of my comment. By that I meant a decision to make each expert good at a human-defined thing.
children.textZhHtml: 注意我评论中“但免除了不必要的归纳偏置”这部分。我的意思是，决定让每个专家都擅长人类定义的特定任务。

children.author: ralusek
children.textRawHtml:
MoE is literally exactly what they're describing. The classifier being described is baked into the model and is the thing that makes it MoE.

Imagine I had [a model that was good at math], [model that was good at code], [model that was good at writing], [model that was good at general knowledge]. If I then had [a model that was good at determining whether the user query would be best served by one of those models and sent it to it, leaving the rest of the models inactive], that is the platonic version of what MoE is. In practice, it works a bit differently. It instead basically restricts the number of pathways that can be utilized in solving problems during training, which allows for "expert neuron groupings" to form and "classifier layers" to form earlier on in the structure, but the effect is the same (better, even, since it allows some overlap between structures of experts). It also allows "routing to an expert" to happen token-by-token rather than at the prompt level.
children.textZhHtml:
MoE字面意义上完全就是他们描述的那样。所描述的分类器已经内置在模型中，正是这个机制使其成为MoE。

假设我有[一个擅长数学的模型]、[一个擅长代码的模型]、[一个擅长写作的模型]、[一个擅长通用知识的模型]。如果我还有一个[擅长判断用户查询最适合由哪个模型处理并将其路由过去，同时让其他模型保持闲置的模型]，这就是MoE最理想化的形态。在实际应用中，它的工作方式略有不同。它基本上是在训练过程中限制了解决问题时可利用的路径数量，从而允许“专家神经元分组”和“分类器层”在结构早期就形成，但效果是一样的（甚至更好，因为它允许专家结构之间有一定的重叠）。它还允许“路由到专家”按词元逐个进行，而不是在提示词级别进行。

children.author: eamag
children.textRawHtml: only there's no [a model that was good at math], [model that was good at code]... in the MoE
children.textZhHtml: 只是在MoE中并没有[擅长数学的模型]、[擅长代码的模型]……

children.author: kennywinker
children.textRawHtml: There is value in splitting things. If all I ever do is local app automations, i don’t need model that knows how to code. If all I ever do is coding, i don’t need a model that translates english to slovakian.
children.textZhHtml: 任务拆分是有价值的。如果我做的只是本地应用自动化，我就不需要一个懂编程的模型。如果我做的只是编程，我就不需要一个能把英语翻译成斯洛伐克语的模型。

children.author: ArcHound
children.textRawHtml: Slovakia mentioned, let's gooo. Ehm, exactly, we can achieve better smaller models for specialized tasks rather than using compute to improve a big model that does everything. There's a lingering philosophical question if better language processing capabilities translate to better image processing capabilities (i.e. having the vocabulary and experience to properly describe an image), but I still think that identifying tasks and splitting responsibilities saves a lot of effort.
children.textZhHtml: 提到斯洛伐克了，冲啊。呃，没错，我们可以为专门的任务打造更好的小模型，而不是把算力都用来提升一个什么都能干的大模型。这里还有一个悬而未决的哲学问题：更强的语言处理能力是否能转化为更强的图像处理能力（即拥有准确描述图像的词汇量和经验），但我仍然认为，明确任务并拆分职责能省下大量精力。

children.author: [deleted]
children.textRawHtml: [deleted]
children.textZhHtml: [已删除]

children.author: bigmadshoe
children.textRawHtml: Good point! I thought you meant splitting them and then doing inference with some kind of learned router while keeping all the split models loaded at once. What you're suggesting is pretty sensible.
children.textZhHtml: 说得好！我原以为你的意思是把它们拆分，然后用某种学习到的路由器进行推理，同时保持所有拆分后的模型同时加载。你的建议非常合理。

children.author: Zababa
children.textRawHtml: There is value in splitting things but there is also a cost. You have to train the specialized model, for that you have to know your use case, you have to hope the use case is going to be stable over time, you then have to see if you can remove english -> slovakian or coding from a model without affecting the useful parts.
children.textZhHtml: 拆分模型有其价值，但也有代价。你必须训练专用模型，为此你得明确自己的使用场景，还得指望这个场景在未来一段时间内保持稳定，然后你还得看看能否从模型中移除英语到斯洛伐克语的翻译或编程能力，而不影响其他有用的部分。

children.author: graysonk
children.textRawHtml:
I enjoy doing local image generation and this is one thing that the community around that has really optimized.

In some workflows you might have 20 different models doing their specialized tasks. Pose detection, hand/eye/face detailers, classifiers, refiners, up scalers, taggers, etc can all use their own models and that’s not even the including the model(s) used for the actual image generation part.

I’m interested to see the optimization when this concept gets applied to other general ai tasks.
children.textZhHtml:
我喜欢进行本地图像生成，这是社区真正优化的一件事。

在某些工作流程中，您可能有 20 个不同的模型来执行其专门的任务。姿势检测、手/眼/面部细节器、分类器、细化器、放大器、标记器等都可以使用自己的模型，这甚至不包括用于实际图像生成部分的模型。

当这个概念应用于其他一般人工智能任务时，我有兴趣看到优化。

children.author: dofm
children.textRawHtml:
FWIW my tests on my little puzzles suggest that it is not better than the Gemma 4 12B on SQL. It really does seem to get quite tangled up on stuff.

PHP/Wordpress code seems OK (better than the Gemma) but it gets stuck in reasoning loops.

Mind you, I am something of a cynic about the underlying 27B dense Qwen; I think the 35B MoE model is often better and it is just so, so much faster.
children.textZhHtml:
FWIW 我对小谜题的测试表明它并不比 SQL 上的 Gemma 4 12B 更好。它看起来确实很混乱。

PHP/Wordpress 代码看起来不错（比 Gemma 更好），但它陷入了推理循环。

请注意，我对底层 27B 密集 Qwen 有点愤世嫉俗；我认为 35B MoE 模型通常更好，而且速度更快。

children.author: sosodev
children.textRawHtml: Qwen3.6-27B is the best model in that range that I’ve used for agentic coding by far. I think it’s kinda mid at everything else.
children.textZhHtml: 在我用过的同参数量级模型中，Qwen3.6-27B绝对是智能体编程方面表现最好的。不过我觉得它在其他方面也就中规中矩。

children.author: larodi
children.textRawHtml:
Surely not that good at vision. TBH none of these 14-27b models come close to even the cheapest Gemma 2.5 flash.

If these buddies are similarly bad on text, then they definitely don’t get anywhere close to big boys, no matter what the synthetic stats claim upon release.
children.textZhHtml:
视觉能力肯定没那么好。说实话，这些14-27b的模型里，没一个能比得上最便宜的Gemma 2.5 flash。

如果这些家伙在文本方面也同样拉胯，那它们绝对跟那些大模型没法比，不管发布时那些合成数据吹得有多好。

children.author: tharkun__
children.textRawHtml:
From my own experiments with local, low VRAM model use vs. what I'm used to from using Claude at work is that being good at "coding" is of no use, if you're worse at "tool calling" as coding in an agentic way requires quite a bit of tool calling.

If you can "hide" different models of 8GB VRAM requirements each that have those specialties and mix and match them for me without having to manage it manually, I'll be impressed. Until then I will keep using my Claude, because "remarkably good _for their size_" models I've tried so far just sucked at trying to use them the way I code at work with Claude.
children.textZhHtml:
从我自己对本地低 VRAM 模型使用的实验与我在工作中习惯使用 Claude 的情况来看，如果你不擅长“工具调用”，那么擅长“编码”是没有用的，因为以代理方式编码需要相当多的工具调用。

如果你可以“隐藏”不同的 8GB VRAM 需求模型，每个模型都有这些特性，并为我混合和匹配它们，而无需手动管理，我会印象深刻。在那之前，我将继续使用我的 Claude，因为到目前为止我尝试过的“对于它们的尺寸来说非常好”的模型只是尝试以我与 Claude 一起编码的方式使用它们。

children.author: SwellJoe
children.textRawHtml: To be fair, everything (roughly within an order of magnitude in size) is worse on vision. 12b is a beast for vision tasks, better than its bigger siblings, even.
children.textZhHtml: 公平地说，一切（规模大致在同一数量级）在视觉任务上的表现都要差一些。12B在视觉任务上简直是个性能怪兽，甚至比体量更大的兄弟模型还要出色。

children.author: hypercube33
children.textRawHtml: More to the argument that we need a model of models - one general one that calls specialists in to do what they are good at and handles that like a foreman for you.
children.textZhHtml: 更重要的是，我们需要一个模型中的模型 - 一个通用的模型，可以召集专家来做他们擅长的事情，并像工头一样为您处理这些事情。

stories.title: I tricked Claude into leaking your deepest, darkest secrets
stories.titleZh: 我骗了Claude，让它泄露了你心底最深、最不可告人的秘密。

comments.author: sonink
comments.textRawHtml:
Its a bit wild to me that there hasnt been a pushback against enabling memories by frontier AI companies. This data is something advertisers could only dream off. Before AI, most of this data was approximated by whatever little information could be gleaned from the websites we visit. But now people are handing over their deepest darkest secrets and pretty much EVERYTHING to AI on a platter.

Maybe its just me who is paranoid because I happen to spend a fair bit of time in the advertising world, but the first thing I did when memory was launched on Claude/Chatgpt - was to switch them off. And it helps that they are not even useful, and would actually downgrade your experience by polluting the context of irrelevant details. I go one step ahead - if there is a personal discussion you want to have - maybe use another account like provided by the likes of companies like openrouter etc.

I would argue that we should have regulation that should prohibit the storage of user profile information by AI companies, and any such memories feature should exclusively reside on the users servers. Infact, maybe go one step ahead, that 'memory' firms cannot be owned by AI firms and vice versa.
comments.textZhHtml:
前沿人工智能公司居然没有反对启用记忆功能，这让我有点疯狂。这些数据是广告商梦寐以求的。在人工智能出现之前，大部分数据都是通过从我们访问的网站中收集到的少量信息来近似的。但现在人们正在把他们最深的最黑暗的秘密和几乎所有的东西都交给人工智能。

也许只有我是偏执的，因为我碰巧在广告界花了相当多的时间，但当记忆在 Claude/Chatgpt 上启动时，我做的第一件事就是关掉它们。而且它们甚至没有用，而且实际上会通过污染不相关细节的上下文来降低您的体验，这很有帮助。我先行一步 - 如果你想进行个人讨论 - 也许可以使用其他帐户，例如 openrouter 等公司提供的帐户。

我认为我们应该制定法规，禁止人工智能公司存储用户个人资料信息，并且任何此类记忆功能都应专门驻留在用户服务器上。事实上，也许向前迈出一步，“记忆”公司不能由人工智能公司拥有，反之亦然。

children.author: jayGlow
children.textRawHtml: I've found memory somewhat useful as i don't need to give it all the context for everything, that said it's equally as annoying when it latches onto something I said in a different chat and derails the conversation because of that.
children.textZhHtml: 我发现记忆有些有用，因为我不需要为它提供所有内容的所有上下文，也就是说，当它锁定我在另一个聊天中所说的内容并因此破坏对话时，它同样令人烦恼。

children.author: 4gotunameagain
children.textRawHtml:
Data harvesting is one of the core value propositions for many of these companies (from the investor's perspective).

Companies that built their models on public data and illegal scraping/copyrighted works, amassing massive datasets on the most private aspects of countless individuals, and creating a huge bubble with potentially humongous implications upon implosion. Oh, and, increasing wealth concentration and inequality by an incredible amount.

The future is here.
children.textZhHtml:
数据收集是许多此类公司的核心价值主张之一（从投资者的角度来看）。

这些公司在公共数据和非法抓取/受版权保护的作品上建立了自己的模型，积累了无数个人最隐私方面的大量数据集，并创造了一个巨大的泡沫，一旦内爆，可能会产生巨大的影响。哦，而且，财富集中度和不平等程度正以令人难以置信的程度增加。

未来就在这里。

children.author: daheza
children.textRawHtml: I think there will eventually be pushback from companies that want to keep their IP secrets. The current standard of "we dont train on your data" but we summarize all your input and output, meaning its not your data anymore and we can train on that.
children.textZhHtml: 我认为想要保守知识产权秘密的公司最终会遭到抵制。当前的标准是“我们不会根据您的数据进行训练” 但我们总结了您的所有输入和输出，这意味着它不再是您的数据，我们可以对其进行训练。

comments.author: artisinal
comments.textRawHtml:
Doesn’t surprise me.

Yesterday I learned that people run AI agents on their system with full admin rights. No containerisation or anything. Wild. Like we forgot 50 years of computer security overnight.
comments.textZhHtml:
我并不感到惊讶。

昨天我了解到人们在他们的系统上运行具有完全管理权限的人工智能代理。没有集装箱化或任何东西。荒野。就像我们一夜之间忘记了 50 年的计算机安全一样。

children.author: progval

children.author: cowpig
children.textRawHtml:
It's much different.

The dependency trees have a whole system that's evolved for decades. The same code goes into many computers. Many people read the source, security firms look for vulnerabilities, etc.

Language models are a completely new paradigm. The code it writes on your machine is the only instance of that code. It does far more than anybody could ever keep track of.

It's much harder to detect problems, and nobody to hold accountable for them.
children.textZhHtml:
这完全不同。

依赖树已经发展了几十年，形成了一整套体系。相同的代码会部署到许多计算机上。许多人会阅读源码，安全公司会寻找漏洞等等。

语言模型则是一个全新的范式。它在你机器上写出的代码是该代码的唯一实例。它生成的代码量远超任何人所能追踪的范围。

发现问题要困难得多，而且出了问题也没人负责。

children.author: XorNot
children.textRawHtml:

children.textZhHtml:
在 Linux 上创建独立用户从来都不容易，尤其是在需要在不同上下文之间切换的任务中更是如此。

Docker 是长期以来在这方面迈出的最大步伐之一。

children.textRawHtml: I meant for CLI tasks. Just "adduser" and "sudo -u <user> bash".
children.textZhHtml: 我的意思是针对 CLI 任务。直接“adduser”然后“sudo -u <user> bash”就行了。

children.author: brookst
children.textRawHtml:
And when you want to share some but not all files with that one user but not other users you created for similar purposes?

And when you want the outputs of that user back to your main user?

And when you want that user to access some shared credentials for external services, but not all?

It’s not the account setup that’s hard, it’s the workflow of spreading a single real-world across multiple accounts.
children.textZhHtml:
当你想和那个用户共享部分而非全部文件，却又不想共享给其他出于类似目的创建的用户时怎么办？

当你想把那个用户的输出结果传回你的主用户时怎么办？

当你想让那个用户访问外部服务的部分共享凭证，而不是全部时怎么办？

难的并不是账户设置，而是如何将单一的现实工作流分散到多个账户中的工作流程。

children.author: boutell
children.textRawHtml:
Thank you. Among other important points there:

"That said, Japan isn't the only country pursuing lithium recovery. In the US, Redwood Materials – the recycling company founded by former Tesla CTO JB Straubel – says it's already recovering 95% of lithium from the equivalent of about 250,000 EVs per year."

EV batteries are too large and valuable to wind up in landfills, although I'm sure it has happened somewhere. I do like that Japan is is making it harder to just throw out smaller lithium batteries. That strikes me as a more probable source of waste.
children.textZhHtml:
谢谢。其中还有其他几个重要观点：

“话虽如此，日本并不是唯一追求锂回收的国家。在美国，由特斯拉前首席技术官JB Straubel创立的回收公司Redwood Materials表示，它每年已经从相当于约25万辆电动汽车的电池中回收了95%的锂。”

电动汽车电池体积大、价值高，不太可能最终被扔进垃圾填埋场，尽管我敢肯定某个地方肯定发生过。我确实很欣赏日本让随意丢弃小型锂电池变得更难的做法。在我看来，这才是更有可能产生浪费的来源。

children.author: pixl97
children.textRawHtml: I would add that EV batteries are too flammable to end up in landfills. A few small batteries from things like phones and laptops generally make it through the waste processing chain without rupturing and causing problems, but a large pile of them outside of containment starts becoming a certainty that one will break and set the whole lot on fire.
children.textZhHtml: 我想补充一点，电动汽车电池的易燃性太高，不能直接扔进垃圾填埋场。像手机和笔记本电脑里的一些小电池通常能顺利通过废物处理流程而不会破裂引发问题，但如果是一大堆电池堆放在没有防护的地方，那几乎肯定会有电池破损并引发大火。

comments.author: cyphar
comments.textRawHtml:
It really should not be surprising that we can get very high recovery percentages from batteries -- we do not mine elemental lithium, so the processes we use for extraction are already designed to extract lithium from fairly low-purity sources. In contrast, lithium batteries are an incredibly high-purity source of lithium. The main question is when it will become cost-effective to create recycling pipelines.

Lead acid batteries had a similar trajectory and modern lead acid batteries are effectively 100% recycled.
comments.textZhHtml:
我们能从电池中获得极高的回收率，这其实并不令人惊讶——我们并不开采单质锂，所以我们使用的提取工艺本来就是为从纯度相当低的来源中提取锂而设计的。相比之下，锂电池是纯度极高的锂来源。主要问题在于，何时建立回收流水线才能实现成本效益。

铅酸电池也经历了类似的发展轨迹，现代铅酸电池实际上实现了100%回收。

children.author: legitster
children.textRawHtml:
This is probably somewhat true, but also I suspect there might be an order of magnitude difference from extracting trace lithium from inert rock vs collecting it as a salt from amongst a medley of very refined metals.

Case in point - lead acid batteries are not a fair comparison. A lead acid battery is so robust you can separate the cathodes and anodes with your (gloved) hands. Getting the elements out of a lead battery is like picking pieces of pepperoni off of a pizza. Whereas taking lithium out of a lithium cell is like pulling only a certain protein out of a roll of bologna. And the protein catches fire in contact with air.
children.textZhHtml:
这可能有一定道理，但我也怀疑，从惰性岩石中提取微量锂与从一堆高度精炼的金属中将其作为盐类收集起来，两者之间可能存在一个数量级的差异。

举个例子——铅酸电池不是一个公平的比较对象。铅酸电池非常坚固，你甚至可以戴着手套用双手把阴极和阳极分开。从铅电池中提取元素就像从披萨上挑出意大利辣香肠片一样简单。而从锂电池中提取锂，则就像从一卷博洛尼亚香肠中只抽出某种特定的蛋白质。而且这种蛋白质一接触空气就会燃烧。
